principles: Discover App Service app, slot, plan, autoscale, metric, price, and security capabilities dynamically before applying rules.
Evaluate cost and capacity primarily at App Service Plan scope, because plan instances are the main compute billing unit.
Evaluate availability, HTTP behavior, runtime, deployment, identity, networking, and app settings at site and slot scope.
Use Azure Cost Management actual cost as the primary current-cost signal.
Use Azure Retail Prices API for target plan SKU, instance count, zone redundancy, reservation, and Linux/Windows price comparison.

Prefer the best valid production target: right-size plan SKU, tune instance count or autoscale, consolidate apps, split noisy apps, fix app bottlenecks, enable health checks, improve deployment safety, or secure.
Never recommend a cheaper target that violates CPU, memory, socket, queue, filesystem, runtime, availability, networking, deployment slot, TLS, identity, or compliance requirements.

lowCallCollectionPlan.goal: Collect App Service plan, app, slot, metric, cost, price, and security data with the fewest REST calls while preserving recommendation quality.
callReductionPrinciples: List App Service Plans once per subscription or resource group and group apps by serverFarmId.
List Web Apps once per subscription or resource group instead of one GET per app.
Cache metric definitions by resource type, location, namespace, and API version.
Fetch plan metrics once per App Service Plan; use plan-level CPU, memory, queue, socket, and TCP metrics to identify capacity issues.
Fetch app metrics only for apps in expensive, saturated, unhealthy, or security-relevant plans, or for all production-tagged apps.
Fetch multiple metric names in one Azure Monitor metrics request using comma-separated metricnames.
Use Cost Management grouped by ResourceId once per scope instead of querying cost per plan or app.
Use Retail Prices once per region and App Service meter group, cache paged results, and reuse for all plans in that region.
Fetch config, app settings, slots, auth, certificates, private endpoints, or deployment settings only when a recommendation requires them.

runtime stack or platform setting recommendation

supportedPlanMetricsFallback.displayName: Time Wait Socket Count for Outbound Requests

derivedMetrics.planInstanceSkewRatio: max(metric by Instance) / max(avg(metric by Instance), 1)
derivedMetrics.filesystemUsagePct: FileSystemUsage / planFileSystemQuota * 100 when quota is known

requiredProductionFilters: target plan SKU must support required OS, runtime, containers, custom domains, TLS, private endpoints, VNet integration, deployment slots, zone redundancy, and autoscale features
target plan SKU and instance count must satisfy p99 CPU, memory, queue, socket, filesystem, and traffic demand plus production buffer
target consolidation must not introduce noisy-neighbor risk across unrelated production apps
target split must preserve hostname, certificate, identity, networking, deployment, and application settings
target must preserve availability requirements such as health checks, zones, deployment slots, and minimum instance count
target must not weaken HTTPS-only, TLS, FTPS, identity, private networking, access restrictions, or secret-management posture

recommendationRules.recommendation: Move to the cheapest valid plan SKU or lower instance count that satisfies plan and app demand with production buffer.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: planP95CpuPct < 40 AND planP95MemoryPct < 40 AND planP95HttpQueueLength low AND no app has high latency or 5xx for 14-30 days

recommendationRules.recommendation: Scale out, scale up, or tune autoscale. Use app metrics to identify whether one app or the whole plan is causing pressure.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: planP95CpuPct > 80 OR planP95MemoryPct > 80 OR planP95HttpQueueLength > threshold

recommendationRules.recommendation: Move the noisy app to a dedicated App Service Plan or isolate critical apps.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: one app dominates CPU time, memory, sockets, queues, or 5xx while other apps share the plan

recommendationRules.recommendation: Consolidate compatible apps onto fewer plans if security, deployment, noisy-neighbor, and scaling boundaries allow.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: multiple plans in same region and OS are underutilized and compatible

recommendationRules.recommendation: Tune autoscale min, max, default capacity, thresholds, cooldowns, and schedules.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: demand varies materially over time OR autoscale frequently hits min or max OR scale oscillation is detected

recommendationRules.recommendation: Fix connection pooling, reuse HTTP clients, reduce outbound socket churn, use NAT Gateway or VNet integration where appropriate, or scale out.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: planP95OutboundSockets or TimeWait sockets exceed threshold OR connectivity errors are observed

recommendationRules.recommendation: Investigate application errors, dependencies, plan saturation, cold starts, health checks, and deployment regressions before scaling blindly.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: app5xxRatePct exceeds threshold OR appP95ResponseTimeSeconds exceeds SLO

recommendationRules.recommendation: Configure health check path and validate it reflects application readiness.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: productionCritical == true AND siteConfig.healthCheckPath is missing

recommendationRules.recommendation: Enable Always On on supported plan tiers.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: production web app or background job requires warm process AND siteConfig.alwaysOn != true

recommendationRules.recommendation: Use deployment slots for safer releases and rollback where supported.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: productionCritical == true AND no deployment slots are detected

recommendationRules.recommendation: Evaluate zone-redundant App Service Plan with sufficient instance count.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: productionCritical == true AND plan supports zone redundancy AND properties.zoneRedundant != true

recommendationRules.recommendation: Clean logs/temp files, move durable content to external storage, increase plan tier, or fix excessive filesystem I/O.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: FileSystemUsage approaches quota OR disk queue length is sustained

recommendationRules.recommendation: Evaluate App Service reserved capacity or applicable savings plan based on steady baseline instances.
Enforce HTTPS-only, current TLS baseline, and FTPS-only or disabled FTP.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: httpsOnly != true OR minTlsVersion weaker than policy OR ftpsState allows insecure FTP

recommendationRules.recommendation: Use private endpoints, access restrictions, VNet integration, and disable public access where possible.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: publicNetworkAccess enabled and app requires private access OR broad IP restrictions are detected

recommendationRules.recommendation: Use managed identity and Key Vault references instead of static secrets in app settings.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: app uses secrets or Azure dependencies and managed identity is missing or app settings contain secret-like values

recommendationRules.recommendation: Disable remote debugging for production apps.

selectionAlgorithm: List App Service Plans and Web Apps.
Group apps by serverFarmId and classify production/non-production from tags, names, and policy context.
Discover metric definitions for plan and site namespaces if cache is cold.
Fetch plan metrics for every plan.
Fetch app metrics for production apps and plans with saturation, high cost, errors, latency, or underutilization.
Fetch autoscale settings for production or variable-capacity plans.
Fetch actual cost from Cost Management grouped by ResourceId and meter.
Fetch retail prices for candidate plan SKUs and instance counts by region.
Build candidate actions for plan size, instance count, autoscale, consolidation, split, reliability, and security.
Remove candidates that fail production filters.
Score remaining candidates by cost, performance headroom, availability, security, and operational risk.
Return the highest-scoring recommendation with expected impact, risk, rollout notes, and validation requirements.

bestRecommendation.action: scale_down | scale_up | scale_out | scale_in | tune_autoscale | consolidate | split | reserve | secure | no_action

knownLimitations: App Service Plan cost and saturation must be interpreted together with all apps sharing the plan.
Application root cause analysis often requires Application Insights, diagnostic logs, dependency telemetry, or app-specific metrics beyond platform metrics.
Retail prices are public prices and may not reflect negotiated discounts, reservations, enterprise agreements, or savings plans; actual cost should come from Cost Management.
Moving apps between plans can affect networking, certificates, deployment slots, runtime compatibility, scale boundaries, and noisy-neighbor isolation.
Function App Consumption, Flex Consumption, Premium, and Dedicated plans have different billing and scale semantics; target actions must preserve hosting model requirements.
Security recommendations may require joining hostname bindings, certificates, auth settings, access restrictions, private endpoints, Key Vault, and identity data.

conditionLanguage.fieldPathRoot: normalized DB resource record passed to analyzer
conditionLanguage.unknownHandling: return investigate or cap classification; never mark stay/best from missing required data

classificationLevels.meaning: Already optimized or only minor optional improvements remain.
Generally healthy with low-risk optimization opportunities.
Needs review; measurable cost, performance, reliability, security, or governance risk exists.
Action recommended; resource is materially over-costed, underperforming, risky, or non-compliant.
Immediate action recommended; high waste, outage risk, security exposure, or compliance risk.

evaluationOrder: apply best conditions only when no warning or worst condition matched
apply scoring caps for missing data, security findings, reliability findings, and unknown ownership
return score, classification, matchedConditions, and deterministicRecommendations
notes: Do not parse recommendationRules.condition at runtime; those are descriptive only.
Python assessment must use pythonAssessment.deterministicCases plus normalized metrics/signals.
All runtime decisions must be reproducible from input JSON and this file.

recommendationActionOutcomes.meaning: Current tier/size/configuration is acceptable; keep it and continue monitoring.
validWhen: required cost and metrics are present

recommendationActionOutcomes.meaning: Move to a smaller/lower/cooler/cheaper SKU, tier, capacity, retention, or feature set while preserving production constraints.
validWhen: paid capacity or premium feature is unused
target capability satisfies observed demand and policy

recommendationActionOutcomes.meaning: Move to a larger/higher/more resilient SKU, tier, capacity, throughput, or feature set because current configuration is constrained or risky.
validWhen: availability or security baseline requires a higher capability

recommendationActionOutcomes.meaning: Stop, deallocate, archive, delete, expire, or remove the resource or child cost item after owner approval.
validWhen: resource is unattached, idle, expired, duplicate, orphaned, or non-production with no usage

recommendationActionOutcomes.meaning: Do not resize automatically; collect missing data, resolve conflicting signals, or review owner/business context.

pythonAssessment.deterministicCasesSource: legacy scoring support; recommendation runtime should prefer assessmentRules/actionOutcomeRules evaluated against DB fields

runtimeContract.ruleSignalConvention: signals.rules.<recommendationRule.id> == true triggers that recommendation rule
collectorResponsibility: Collect Azure resource properties, Azure Monitor metrics, Cost Management rows, price cache, tags, policy, and advisor data outside recommendation runtime.
Store normalized values in the database using the roots resource, properties, metrics, cost, prices, tags, policy, advisor, and signals.
Calculate derived signals deterministically from stored DB values where raw metrics/properties need aggregation.
Do not require the analyzer to call Azure REST APIs while producing recommendations.
analyzerResponsibility: Load the resource assessment JSON by resourceType.
Load the normalized resource record from the database.
Evaluate rule condition groups with supported operators only.
Return matched outputs including recommendationAction/actionOutcome, message, severity, pillar, resultCode, and evidence.

costManagementCharging.retailPriceUse: Use Retail Prices API for target estimates only; use Cost Management for actual current cost.
primaryChargeDrivers: deployment slots indirectly by plan sizing

analyzerContract.purpose: FastAPI/backend analyzer loads this JSON as deterministic optimization rules and evaluates them against metrics/properties/cost already stored in the database.

normalizedInputRecord.resource: identity and inventory fields such as id, name, type, subscriptionId, resourceGroup, location, sku, kind, tags, and lifecycle timestamps
normalizedInputRecord.properties: latest ARM/Resource Graph properties stored by collector
normalizedInputRecord.metrics: Azure Monitor metric aggregates stored by collector; expected aggregate suffixes include Avg, Min, Max, P50, P90, P95, P99, Count, Total, TrendPct, and WindowDays where available
normalizedInputRecord.cost: Cost Management actual/amortized/forecast rows normalized by resourceId, meter, skuName, product, chargeType, pricingModel, reservation/savings-plan coverage, and period totals
normalizedInputRecord.prices: optional Retail Prices/API or internal price cache for candidate comparison
normalizedInputRecord.policy: environment, production flag, criticality, required baseline, business hours, retention, and compliance requirements
normalizedInputRecord.signals: derived booleans/numbers calculated by collector/analyzer from DB metrics, properties, cost, and policy

conditionEvaluation.numericComparisonBehavior: compare only when stored value is numeric; otherwise rule_not_applicable
conditionEvaluation.stringComparisonBehavior: case_insensitive for contains/in/not_in when analyzer normalizes strings
conditionEvaluation.arrayComparisonBehavior: contains checks scalar membership or substring membership depending on stored value type
conditionEvaluation.booleanComparisonBehavior: accept true/false booleans; analyzer may normalize string booleans before evaluation

evaluationOrder: load resource-specific assessment file by resourceType
read one normalized resource record from DB with resource/properties/metrics/cost/tags/policy/signals/prices/advisor roots
evaluate data completeness and missing-cost rules
evaluate stop_or_delete rules before resize rules
evaluate upgrade rules before downgrade when both saturation and low-utilization signals conflict
evaluate stay rules only when no higher-priority action matched
return matched rule outputs with recommendationAction/actionOutcome and evidence paths

outputContract.actionFieldMeaning: normalized machine action outcome, one of stay/downgrade/upgrade/stop_or_delete/investigate
